reference_answer: Robert Gaudette. The document gives his prior role as "the Company's current Executive Vice President, NRG Business and Wholesale Operations." (Item 5.02, first paragraph.)

April 30, 2026 — described as "the date of the Company's next annual meeting of stockholders." (Item 5.02, first paragraph.) Note: his President role takes effect immediately on January 6, 2026; the CEO role is forward-dated.
Chair of the Board, succeeding Dr. Coben. (Item 5.02, second paragraph.) Mr. Carrillo has served on the Board since 2019.
001-15891. (Cover-page jurisdiction table; middle cell with the inline label "(Commission File Number)".)
Christine A. Zoino, Corporate Secretary. (Signature block, dated January 7, 2026.)

Item 5.02 ("Departure of Directors or Certain Officers; Election of Directors; Appointment of Certain Officers; Compensatory Arrangements of Certain Officers"). All three executives' transitions are covered in the same Item 5.02 narrative paragraphs.

The Company's definitive proxy statement on Schedule 14A, filed with the SEC on March 19, 2025. (Item 5.02, third paragraph.) Note the document contains a small textual gap: "Biographical and other information about Mr. Gaudette is included the Company's definitive proxy statement" — the word "in" appears to be missing between "included" and "the." Preserve verbatim.
Item 7.01 (Regulation FD): "On January 7, 2026, the Company issued a press release announcing the matters described in Item 5.02 hereof." Press release is Exhibit 99.1, dated January 7, 2026.

The second row contains "NYSE Texas" in the "Trading Symbol(s)" column. The "Title of each class" and "Name of each exchange on which registered" cells are empty. The intended reading appears to be that NRG common stock is also registered on NYSE Texas, but the table row is incomplete — the title and exchange-name cells are blank where the row implies they should repeat or specify content. An additional standalone line "NYSE Texas [Member]" appears near the top of the document outside the table, suggesting the same registration is referenced twice with different formatting.

Item 5.02, fourth paragraph: "As of the filing of this report, the Compensation Committee of the Board and the Board have not finalized the compensation of Mr. Gaudette in connection with his appointment as Chief Executive Officer and President. The Company will provide this information by filing an amendment to this Report after the information becomes available."

All four are unchecked (¨): - Written communications pursuant to Rule 425 under the Securities Act; - Soliciting material pursuant to Rule 14a-12 under the Exchange Act; - Pre-commencement communications pursuant to Rule 14d-2(b) under the Exchange Act; - Pre-commencement communications pursuant to Rule 13e-4(c) under the Exchange Act. Note: the cover page also shows two unchecked check-mark fields for emerging-growth-company status.

- Robert Gaudette — current Executive Vice President, NRG Business and Wholesale Operations; appointed President effective January 6, 2026; appointed Chief Executive Officer effective April 30, 2026; to be named director nominee at the 2026 Annual Meeting. - Lawrence Coben (Dr. Coben) — President (until January 6, 2026), Chief Executive Officer and Chair of the Board (through April 30, 2026), advisor (April 30, 2026 through end of 2026 fiscal year). - Antonio Carrillo — Board member since 2019; appointed Chair of the Board effective April 30, 2026. - Christine A. Zoino — Corporate Secretary, signatory.

- January 6, 2026 — date of earliest event reported; Board appointments and Coben notification. - April 30, 2026 — Annual Meeting; Gaudette CEO effective date; Coben step-down date; Carrillo Chair effective date. - End of the 2026 fiscal year — endpoint of Coben's advisory period. - March 19, 2025 — filing date of definitive proxy statement on Schedule 14A. - January 7, 2026 — press release date; 8-K signature date.

- Exhibit 99.1: Press Release, dated January 7, 2026. - Exhibit 104: Cover Page Interactive Data File (formatted as Inline XBRL). No Exhibit 10.X — there is no separation or employment agreement attached, consistent with the disclosure that compensation has not yet been finalized.

- "Mr. Gaudette does not have any family relationships with any director or executive officer of the Company"; - "there are no arrangements or understandings with any persons pursuant to which Mr. Gaudette has been appointed to his position"; - "there have been no transactions directly or indirectly involving Mr. Gaudette that would be required to be disclosed pursuant to Item 404(a) of Regulation S-K."

Dr. Coben's resignation as President is effective immediately (i.e., as of January 6, 2026). He continues to hold both Chief Executive Officer and Chair of the Board through April 30, 2026. So between January 6 and April 30, 2026, he is CEO + Chair but not President — and his successor as President (Gaudette) is in place during that same window.

Start: April 30, 2026. End: end of the 2026 fiscal year. The document does not specify whether NRG's fiscal year ends December 31 or some other date; the natural reading for a U.S. public company like NRG is calendar year, but the 8-K does not explicitly say so. Qualified answer: end date is "end of the 2026 fiscal year" as written; calendar value not stated within the document.

The 8-K commits to disclosure "by filing an amendment to this Report after the information becomes available." No specific date is given. The disclosure is therefore pending and will appear in a future 8-K/A. (Forward-looking obligation; not yet satisfied as of this filing.)

1. (a) and (b) are simultaneous, both effective immediately on January 6, 2026 — Gaudette becomes President at the same moment Coben resigns as President. 2. (c), (d), (e), and (f) are all simultaneous, all effective April 30, 2026: Gaudette becomes CEO; Coben ceases to be CEO (and ceases to be Chair, and departs the Board); Carrillo becomes Chair; Coben becomes advisor. Two clusters separated by ~16 weeks; the cluster-internal ordering is "simultaneous" not "sequential."

The Company is obligated to file an amendment to this Report (a Form 8-K/A) disclosing Mr. Gaudette's CEO and President compensation arrangements once the Compensation Committee and the Board finalize them. The current filing affirmatively notes the omission.

The document affirmatively states: "Dr. Coben's departure is not the result of any disagreement with the Company on any matter relating to the Company's operations, policies, or practices." This is the standard SEC negative-fact disclosure required when a director or executive departs. The document frames the departure as part of a planned succession, but does not otherwise explain the underlying reasons.

"Mr. Gaudette will also be named as a director nominee seeking election at the Annual Meeting." (Item 5.02, first paragraph.) His Board service is therefore conditional on shareholder election at the April 30, 2026 Annual Meeting — it is not automatic. The 8-K does not pre-suppose election success; it states he will be nominated.

The triggering events under Item 5.02 are the Board's appointment of Gaudette as President (effective immediately, January 6, 2026), the Board's contingent appointment of Gaudette as CEO effective April 30, 2026, Coben's notification of intent to step down, Coben's resignation as President (effective immediately, January 6, 2026), and Carrillo's appointment as Chair effective April 30, 2026 — all dated January 6, 2026, which is therefore the "date of earliest event reported" on the cover page. The 8-K was signed and dated January 7, 2026 (one day later) because companies generally file 8-Ks the next business day after the triggering event. The press release (Exhibit 99.1) was also dated January 7, 2026, consistent with this one-day lag. The filing window for an Item 5.02 8-K is four business days, so January 7 is well within compliance.

- **Robert Gaudette**: Old role = Executive Vice President, NRG Business and Wholesale Operations. New roles = President (effective Jan 6, 2026) and CEO (effective Apr 30, 2026). The 8-K does not state when Gaudette ceases his EVP role; presumably he relinquishes it when he becomes President on January 6, but this is not explicitly stated. - **Lawrence Coben**: Old roles = President (ends Jan 6, 2026), CEO (ends Apr 30, 2026), Chair (ends Apr 30, 2026). New role = advisor (begins Apr 30, 2026 through end of 2026 fiscal year). Between Jan 6 and Apr 30, 2026, Coben holds CEO and Chair but not President — there is no role overlap, but there is a 16-week window during which he holds two of his three former titles. - **Antonio Carrillo**: Old role = Lead Independent Director / Board member since 2019 (the 8-K mentions Board service since 2019 only, not the "Lead Independent Director" designation — though that appears in the related press release). New role = Chair of the Board (effective Apr 30, 2026). The 8-K does not state whether he retains the Lead Independent Director designation. Qualified answer. Whose old role and new role overlap: Gaudette — he holds President (new) and EVP NRG Business and Wholesale Operations (old, until he relinquishes it) potentially concurrently. The document does not resolve this.

The 8-K does not address this gap. It refers readers to the March 19, 2025 proxy for biographical information, but Mr. Gaudette's role at the time of that proxy may have been different from his role at the time of this 8-K (which describes him as "the Company's current Executive Vice President, NRG Business and Wholesale Operations"). The 8-K does not state his role as of the March 2025 proxy, nor when he assumed his current EVP role. A reader following the cross-reference may find biographical information that is now stale with respect to title and responsibilities. The implicit assumption is that personal history (education, prior employers) is stable across the 10-month gap; the explicit responsibility now being announced (President, then CEO) postdates the proxy.
